The WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide (APG) for "Menu Button" pattern explicitly lists:
- Up/Down arrows move focus between menu items
- Home/End move to first/last
- All printable characters optionally type-ahead

Without these, a screen-reader user who enters a role="menu" and tries to use arrows will find nothing happens, which is confusing. However, in practice:
- Tab/Shift+Tab navigates between items (each is a native button/anchor)
- Escape closes the menu
- Focus IS moved into the menu on open
- Clicking a document click closes

So the menu is *functional* via Tab — it just doesn't match the arrow-key expectation that role="menu" creates. This is a real inconsistency but not a blocker. I'll rate it medium because the roles now actively advertise a pattern that isn't fully implemented, creating an expectation mismatch specifically for AT users.

content.text:
Based on my thorough review of the diff and source files, here is my assessment.

**Verification of prior findings:**
- ✅ [high] Search input `aria-label="Search files"` — added (app.ts:79)
- ✅ [medium] Decorative icons `aria-hidden="true"` — added to all inline glyphs + centralized in `makeIcon` (dom-builders.ts:566)
- ✅ [medium] Spinner `role="status"` + `aria-label` — added (render-orchestrator.ts:84-85)
- ✅ [medium] Move/Copy prompts — rewritten to plain `"Move \"<name>\" to:"` (dom-builders.ts:351, 365)
- ⚠️ [medium] Menu semantics — roles + focus-on-open added, but arrow-key navigation still missing (partial fix)

**No functional regressions found:** search debounce/Enter/Escape/clear behaviors remain intact in app.ts; tests relocated to toolbar-handlers.test.ts; the deleted `.bak` file was just a backup.

{"dimension":"ui-ux","applicable":true,"summary":"Reviewed the fixer's changes against the 8 prior UI/UX findings. Four medium/high findings are fully resolved: search input now has aria-label='Search files'; all decorative Bootstrap-Icons glyphs (search icon, clear/close button icons, spinner icon, and all menu-item glyphs via makeIcon) carry aria-hidden='true'; the search spinner wrapper now has role='status' + aria-label='Loading search results'; and the Move/Copy prompts were rewritten to plain action-oriented copy naming the file. No functional regressions were introduced (search debounce/Enter/Escape/clear handlers remain in app.ts; the removed test block was relocated to toolbar-handlers.test.ts; the deleted file was only a .bak backup). One prior finding is only PARTIALLY addressed: the row menu now advertises role='menu'/'menuitem' and moves focus to the first item on open, but it still lacks the arrow-key navigation (Up/Down/Home/End) that the menu pattern requires — now that the roles explicitly promise menu semantics, this expectation gap for screen-reader users is more concrete, not less. The three prior low-severity items (placeholder/icon contrast below 4.5:1, clear-btn missing focus style + sub-24px touch target, browse-route loading affordance) remain unaddressed; they are reported at low for completeness.","findings":[{"id":"menu-arrow-key-navigation","severity":"medium","file":"src/app/dom-builders.ts:160-176 (createMenu onDocKey)","title":"Menu advertises role="menu" but lacks arrow-key item navigation (Up/Down/Home/End)","description":"The prior menu-semantics finding (role="menu" advertised but not implemented) was PARTIALLY fixed: createMenu now sets role="menu" on the container, role="menuitem" on each child, and moves focus onto the first item on open (items[0]?.focus()). However, the WAI-ARIA Menu Button keyboard interaction pattern also requires: ArrowDown/ArrowUp to move focus between menu items (wrapping), Home/End to jump to first/last item, and focus staying trapped within the menu while open. The only keydown handler registered (onDocKey) still handles Escape alone. Because role="menu" is now explicitly set, screen readers will announce the dropdown as a menu and users will naturally reach for the arrow keys; finding them inert is a confusing expectation mismatch. (Tab/Shift+Tab still moves between items and Escape closes, so the menu is functional — just not matching the advertised pattern.) This is the remaining gap in the previously-reported medium finding.","fixPrompt":"In src/app/dom-builders.ts, the `createMenu` function builds a `role="menu"` dropdown whose `onDocKey` document listener currently only closes on Escape. Complete the WAI-ARIA Menu Button keyboard pattern so the menu matches its advertised semantics:

1. Inside `onDocKey` (or a new keydown listener attached to the menu element itself when it opens, removed on close), handle ArrowDown, ArrowUp, Home, and End when the menu is open:
   - ArrowDown: move focus to the next `role="menuitem"` child (wrapping from last to first).
   - ArrowUp: move focus to the previous `role="menuitem"` child (wrapping from first to last).
   - Home: move focus to the first item.
   - End: move focus to the last item.
   Determine the currently-focused item via `document.activeElement` and the item list via the `items` array already captured in `createMenu`. Guard so these keys are only handled when the menu is open (not hidden). Call `event.preventDefault()` for the handled arrow/Home/End keys so they don't scroll the page.
2. Keep the existing Escape handling (close the menu) and ensure Escape still works.
3. Add focused tests in src/app/render-browse.test.ts covering: ArrowDown moves focus from the first menu item to the second; ArrowUp wraps from the first item to the last; Home focuses the first item; End focuses the last item. Reference the existing 'opening the menu moves focus onto the first menu item' test for setup patterns (menuBtnOf/actionsOf/menuOf helpers, setupCleared, file/dir fixtures).

Do not change the menu's role attributes, focus-on-open behavior, or Escape handling — only add the arrow/Home/End key handling."},{"id":"clear-btn-focus-and-touch-target","severity":"low","file":"src/app.css:217-240","title":"Clear button still has no visible focus style and is below the 24px touch-target minimum","description":"Unaddressed from the prior review. The `.search-wrapper .clear-btn` defines a `:hover` color change but no `:focus`/`:focus-visible` rule, so keyboard focus on the clear button relies only on the browser default outline — inconsistent with the toolbar text input (blue focus border) and the upload label (explicit :focus-within outline). The button is also only ~20px (16px glyph + padding:2px), below the 24x24 CSS px WCAG 2.5.8 minimum. This is a low-severity polish/consistency issue.","fixPrompt":"In src/app.css, add a visible focus style for the clear button matching the app's accent and enlarge its hit area to at least 24x24 CSS px. Specifically: (1) add a `.search-wrapper .clear-btn:focus-visible` rule with an outline (e.g. `outline: 2px solid var(--accent, #4a9eff); outline-offset: 1px;`) consistent with the toolbar input's focus treatment; (2) increase `.search-wrapper .clear-btn`'s `padding` (currently 2px) and/or set a `min-width`/`min-height` of 24px so the tappable area meets the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum. Keep the glyph visually unchanged."},{"id":"placeholder-icon-contrast","severity":"low","file":"src/app.css:189-198 (color: #9a9a9a)","title":"Search placeholder and in-input icons sit just below the 4.5:1 AA contrast threshold","description":"Unaddressed from the prior review. The search input placeholder and overlaid search/clear icons use `#9a9a9a` on the input background `#333337`, measuring ~4.47:1 — fractionally under the 4.5:1 WCAG AA threshold for normal-size text (placeholder is 13px). The placeholder doubles as the only sighted label/affordance before typing. Low severity.","fixPrompt":"In src/app.css, raise the `#9a9a9a` color used for the search placeholder and in-field icons (`.search-wrapper .search-icon`, `.search-wrapper .clear-btn`, and the placeholder via `::placeholder`) to a value that achieves at least 4.5:1 contrast against the `#333337` input background — e.g. `#a8a8a8` or lighter. Verify the change doesn't reduce the hover color (`#e6e6e6`) differentiation."}]}

Do not change the menu's role attributes, focus-on-open behavior, or Escape handling — only add the arrow/Home/End key handling."},{"id":"browse-route-no-loading-affordance","severity":"low","file":"src/app/render-orchestrator.ts:74-86","title":"Loading spinner shown for search but not for folder navigation, creating inconsistent feedback","description":"Unaddressed from the prior review (and now made explicit in a code comment: 'For browse routes, no spinner is shown'). The spinner is gated behind `if (route.view === 'search')`, so navigating into a folder (a `browse` route) clears the results and fetches with no loading affordance, while searching shows a spinner for the same kind of async fetch. On slow connections this looks like a hang or a frozen listing for browse navigation. `render()` already clears `resultsEl.innerHTML` synchronously for both routes, so the browse path shows a blank panel until the fetch resolves. For consistent perceived performance, show the same spinner (or a neutral one) for browse routes too.","fixPrompt":"In src/app/render-orchestrator.ts, the `render()` function currently shows the `.search-spinner` (role="status", aria-label) only when `route.view === 'search'`. Extend the spinner to ALSO render for `route.view === 'browse'` so folder navigation shows the same loading affordance as search. Concretely: change the gate `if (route.view === 'search')` to `if (route.view === 'search' || route.view === 'browse')` (or remove the gate entirely), and update the aria-label to a neutral message that fits both routes (e.g. 'Loading…') so it is accurate for browse as well as search. Keep role="status" and aria-hidden on the icon. Update the existing 'search loading spinner' tests in src/app/render-orchestration.test.ts to reflect the new label if changed, and add an assertion (or a new test) that the spinner appears during a browse fetch.
